pi_items.source: Verified from setup_task.sh which downloads from https://files.rcsb.org/download/1URN.pdb
Verified against verifier.py and Python-based calculation of PDB geometry yielding ~3.47A and ~3.42A
Verified against verifier.py and Python-based calculation of PDB geometry yielding ~3.47A and ~3.42A
Verified by calculating minimal distance (3.47A) between Tyr13 (Chain A) sidechain and Cytosine 10 (Chain P) base using a custom python script parsing 1URN.pdb
Verified by calculating minimal distance (3.42A) between Phe56 (Chain A) sidechain and Adenine 11 (Chain P) base using a custom python script parsing 1URN.pdb

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real structural data for PDB:1URN. A custom python script analyzing 1URN.pdb confirms that the specific RNA bases pi-stacking against protein Chain A's Tyr13 and Phe56 are indeed Cytosine 10 (at 3.47 Å) and Adenine 11 (at 3.42 Å). Critically, the prompt instructs the agent to search for RNA bases in 'Chain B'. This is factually incorrect; in 1URN, Chain B is another copy of the protein, while the RNA chain interacting with protein Chain A is Chain P. The verifier checks for correctly identifying 'C 10' and 'A 11' regardless of the chain letter.
